rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Co-catalyzed enantioselective C-H activation forming an isolable chiral cobaltacycle intermediate via anodic oxidation
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (RVC anode, CPE @ 1.5 V vs Ag+/Ag) oxidizes Co(II) to higher-valent Co(III)/Co species enabling C-H metalation and formation of the cobaltacycle

key_intermediates.name: Chiral cobaltacycle (RC)-Co-1 with Salox ligand, benzamide substrate, and quinoline coordination
High-valent Co(III) species after anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Co(OAc)2 coordinates with the (R)-Salox ligand L1-7 and benzamide 1a; anodic oxidation at the RVC electrode oxidizes Co(II) to Co(III), which enables concerted metalation-deprotonation (CMD) of the ortho C-H bond (assisted by pivalate) to generate the isolable five-membered chiral cobaltacycle (RC)-Co-1. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: C-H metalation (CMD) at the high-valent Co center

probe_reagents.name: Isolation of cobaltacycle (RC)-Co-1
probe_reagents.observation: Cobaltacycle isolated in 28% yield (50.3 mg) as dark yellow solid, characterized by 1H/13C NMR and HRMS ([M+Na]+ calcd 622.1511, found 622.1507), confirming chiral C-H metalated Co species

notes: Only the synthesis/characterization of the chiral cobaltacycle intermediate via constant potential electrolysis is shown on this page; subsequent mechanistic experiments (catalytic competence, KIE, DFT) may appear elsewhere in the SI but are not present here. NaOPiv serves as the carboxylate base assisting CMD.
